Title: The Searchers
Notes: Greatest western ever made. John Ford at his peak.

Title: True Grit
Notes: Rooster Cogburn is Wayne's best role. Fight me.

Title: The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance
Notes: Wayne and Stewart together. Print the legend.

Title: Rio Bravo
Notes: Dean Martin is surprisingly great in this.

Title: The Shootist
Notes: Wayne's last film. Knowing that makes it hit harder.

Title: Red River
Notes: Wayne as a cattle drive tyrant. Montgomery Clift steals scenes.

Title: El Dorado
Notes: Basically Rio Bravo again but I don't care, it's fun.

Title: Stagecoach
Notes: The one that made him a star. Monument Valley.

Title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
Notes: Three hours and it earns every minute. Morricone's score.

Title: Unforgiven
Notes: Best western of the modern era. Dark and perfect.

Title: The Outlaw Josey Wales
Notes: 'Dyin' ain't much of a living, boy.' Favorite Eastwood line.

Title: A Fistful of Dollars
Notes: Started the Man with No Name trilogy. Raw and stylish.

Title: For a Few Dollars More
Notes: Lee Van Cleef makes this one. Watch pocket scene.

Title: High Plains Drifter
Notes: Supernatural revenge western. Weird and great.

Title: Pale Rider
Notes: Shane remake basically. Solid but not top-tier Eastwood.

Title: Hang 'Em High
Notes: First American-made Eastwood western. Underrated.

Title: Gran Torino
Notes: Not a western but feels like one. Eastwood's modern Shootist.

Title: Tombstone
Notes: Not Wayne or Eastwood but it belongs here. Val Kilmer IS Doc Holliday.

Title: Open Range
Notes: Best gunfight in any western. Costner and Duvall.

Title: Lonesome Dove (miniseries)
Notes: 8 hours of perfection. Watch it every winter with Jolene.
